intro:
I couldn't find any information on Alchemilla gourzae. However, I found information on Alchemilla mollis and Alchemilla alpina. Here's a paragraph about Alchemilla mollis:

Alchemilla mollis, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its rosettes of leaves and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla mollis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alchemilla mollis is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in skincare products and salves to soothe and calm the skin.
